blocks.content: December came fast. Finals. Papers. Kit's film due Monday the ninth — he turned it in at eleven-fifty-eight PM and sat in the editing bay alone after the building emptied and watched it once all the way through and felt, for the first time all semester, like he'd made something real.
like i made something. i don't know if it's good. but it's real
last final is the 14th. driving home the 15th
On Daisy's end, Claire had taped a paper countdown to the dorm room door.

blocks.speaker: Claire
blocks.content: How many days until you see him.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: We haven't — it's not like that. We're not —

blocks.speaker: Claire
blocks.content: Daisy. I have lived with you for three months. I have seen your face when his name comes up on your phone. How many days.
Claire pulled a marker out of her desk and wrote a 6 on the countdown sheet.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: You are going to be my best friend.
